opp.name: TouroUniversity_Intake_Opp_June'26
opp.next_step: Justin to send Zycus' RFP template.
opp.ais_why:
• Touro University's meeting showed 'positive reception to AI contract agent and AP Smartdesk features,' with interest from Maribel Giraldo 'in reducing rogue spend' and highlighted 'Autonomous Contract Agent' capability.
• Merlin Intake is scoped and the Agentic-platform-discussed flag is set to Yes, confirming AI features were engaged with positively.
• The compelling event is a Unimarket renewal roughly a year out, so the positive AI reception is not yet backed by near-term urgency, keeping it curious.

items.quote: Maribel: 'I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education.' Justin could name only Western Governors University on the 2026-06-16 call.
Maribel: 'Would you be able to share pricing even if it's superficial pricing per module?' Justin did not provide it and has not followed up (as of 2026-06-28).

items.area: Economic Buyer and Decision Maker identification
items.quote: No CFO, Provost, VP of Finance, or VP of Operations named or engaged. Only stakeholder is Maribel Giraldo (Director of Procurement Services, Evaluator role).

Maribel described qualitative pain (siloed campuses, low Unimarket CLM adoption) but did not quantify cost, time savings, or ROI. No dollar-value pain, no headcount inefficiency, no cycle-time baseline.
Maribel did not describe how Touro would evaluate and select a new procurement platform, whether a formal RFP is planned, or who else needs to be involved.

sources: SF Description field (qualitative pain only, no numbers)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. Maribel articulated qualitative pain points on the 2026-06-16 discovery call: 'We are just very big and still operating in silos' and noted that Unimarket's CLM module has 'no traction' despite demos, indicating low adoption and siloed procurement operations across Touro's multi-campus structure. She also mentioned that Unimarket 'serves a purpose' but is exploring what else is available in the market. However, she did not quantify cost, time savings, error reduction, or ROI, and Justin did not probe for metrics. There is no dollar-value pain, no headcount inefficiency, no cycle-time baseline. Without a quantified case, there is no fuel for an executive-level business case. The highest-leverage move is to re-engage Maribel with a targeted question: 'What would a 20% reduction in intake cycle time or a $X annual savings in contract leakage be worth to Touro?' and anchor it to a higher-ed peer benchmark (e.g. Western Governors University's realized ROI). Until metrics are quantified, this remains a market-scan conversation, not a deal.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16, Maribel Giraldo (engaged but not advocating)
champion.narrative: Maribel Giraldo is a weak, early-stage champion. She is engaged (hosted the 2026-06-16 discovery call, asked detailed questions about Merlin Intake, pricing, and higher-ed references, and requested follow-up materials including the RFP template, screenshots, and case studies), but her advocacy is limited. She has not introduced Justin to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or any campus-level stakeholder. She has not shared internal documents, pushed back on competitors, or driven urgency. Her role is Evaluator (SF contact role), and her mandate is market research ('I always want to make sure I know what's available'), not a replacement initiative. On the call she expressed skepticism about switching from Unimarket without strong higher-ed proof: 'I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education.' This is a real barrier she is surfacing, but she is not actively working to overcome it on Zycus's behalf—she is assessing whether Zycus clears the bar. She is a coach (helping Justin understand Touro's concerns and criteria) but not yet a champion (advocating for Zycus internally). The move is to develop her into a true champion by addressing her two blockers (higher-ed references and pricing) in the next 7 days, then asking her to set up a multi-stakeholder call with the finance or operations leader who would sponsor the investment.

sources: SF Competitors__c: Coupa; GEP Worldwide; JAGGAER
Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16, Maribel Giraldo (Unimarket incumbent, no other vendors named)
competition.narrative: The primary competitor is Status Quo / Do Nothing, with Unimarket as the entrenched incumbent and Coupa, JAGGAER, and GEP Worldwide as named alternatives. On the 2026-06-16 discovery call, Maribel stated that Unimarket has been deployed for 8-9 years, 'serves a purpose,' and is not driving an active replacement need. She is conducting market research, not a competitive RFP. The Salesforce Competitors__c field lists Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and JAGGAER, but these were not mentioned on the call—they are likely logged from Justin's intel or prior sweeps. The real competition is inertia: Touro has no compelling event, no quantified pain, and no timeline, so the default is to stay with Unimarket unless Zycus can create urgency. Maribel's hesitation about Zycus's limited higher-ed presence ('very little representation within higher education') is the wedge against us, not a competitor's strength. The how-we-win is to flip the reference gap into social proof: deliver 2-3 named multi-campus university customers (beyond Western Governors University) who have replaced legacy P2P systems with Zycus and realized measurable ROI, and position Merlin Intake as a non-disruptive overlay that can run on top of Unimarket to solve the siloed-campus adoption problem without requiring a full rip-and-replace. This removes the risk Maribel fears and makes Zycus the safe, innovative choice.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16, Maribel Giraldo (pain owner)
identify_pain.narrative: Partial pain confirmed, but not owned by a senior leader. On the 2026-06-16 discovery call, Maribel Giraldo (Director of Procurement Services) described three operational pain points: (1) siloed procurement operations across Touro's multi-campus structure ('We are just very big and still operating in silos'), (2) low adoption of Unimarket's CLM module despite demos ('We've done demos for the contract module, but no traction yet'), and (3) lack of visibility into what procurement tools are available in the market ('I always want to make sure I know what's available'). These are real qualitative pains tied to decentralization and underutilized technology, but Maribel did not quantify the cost (e.g. dollars lost to maverick spend, hours wasted on manual intake, contract leakage). She also framed her exploration as proactive market research, not a crisis: Unimarket 'serves a purpose.' The pain is acknowledged but not acute, and it is owned by a mid-level procurement director, not a CFO, Provost, or VP of Operations who would sponsor a platform replacement. Without a senior pain-owner and a quantified case, this pain will not drive a decision. The move is to ask Maribel to introduce Justin to the finance or operations leader who feels the cost of siloed procurement most acutely, and to quantify the pain with her: 'What does fragmented procurement cost Touro annually in time, compliance risk, or spend leakage?'

paper_process.narrative: Paper process is unknown. There is no evidence of contracting mechanics (MSA/SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec review) in Salesforce or on the 2026-06-16 discovery call. Maribel did not mention Touro's contracting requirements, legal approval, or procurement workflow. The Salesforce Mandate__c field is not populated, and there is no contract expiration date (Current_Contract_Expiration__c) for Unimarket that would act as a forcing function. As a public university system, Touro likely has formal procurement policies, legal review, and InfoSec requirements, but none of these have surfaced yet. Without knowing the paper path, we cannot estimate time-to-close or identify blockers (e.g. Board approval, RFP mandates, vendor vetting). The move is to ask Maribel on the next call: 'What does Touro's contracting and legal approval process look like for a SaaS platform, and are there any procurement policies or InfoSec reviews we should plan for?' This will surface the paper-process timeline and any early blockers.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16, Maribel Giraldo (no EB named)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Maribel Giraldo is Director of Procurement Services (contact role: Evaluator) and is the sole engaged stakeholder. On the 2026-06-16 call she described her role as 'making sure I know what's available in the market' and mentioned that any procurement platform decision would require buy-in from campus-level stakeholders ('we are still operating in silos'), but she did not name a CFO, VP of Finance, or Provost who controls the budget or approves a platform investment of this scale. There is no evidence in Salesforce custom fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c) or in the Avoma call that pricing or commercial conversations have reached a budget authority. Without mapping the EB, the deal cannot progress to commercials or Vendor Selected stage. The evidence suggests budget authority likely sits with a university CFO or VP of Operations across Touro's campuses, but that person is not yet engaged. The immediate move is for Justin and his manager (Michael McCarthy) to ask Maribel directly: 'Who at Touro approves a multi-year SaaS investment for enterprise procurement?' and request an introduction.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16, Maribel Giraldo (no process described)
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is unknown. Maribel has not described how Touro would evaluate and select a new procurement platform, and there is no evidence of a formal process in Salesforce or on the 2026-06-16 call. She mentioned that Touro is 'very big and still operating in silos,' which implies that any enterprise decision would require multi-stakeholder alignment across campuses, but she did not outline the steps, timeline, or approval chain. The Salesforce What_is_the_decision_process__c field is not populated, and there is no mention of an RFP timeline, shortlist stage, or committee. The call sequence shows only one discovery call (2026-06-16) with Maribel; no other stakeholders have joined. Without knowing whether this is a formal RFP, a committee-driven shortlist, or an informal scan, we cannot predict stage progression or plan the next milestone. The move is to ask Maribel directly: 'If Touro decides to move forward, what does the evaluation and approval process look like, and who else needs to be involved?' This will clarify whether we are in an early exploration or the start of a structured evaluation.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16, Maribel Giraldo (criteria inferred from her questions and concerns)
decision_criteria.narrative: Partial signal exists. On the 2026-06-16 discovery call, Maribel articulated three implicit evaluation criteria: (1) higher-education customer proof ('very little representation within higher education' is a concern), (2) pricing transparency and modular flexibility ('would you be able to share pricing even if it's superficial pricing per module?'), and (3) ease of adoption across siloed campuses ('we are just very big and still operating in silos... no traction yet' on Unimarket CLM). These are directional but not formalized. There is no RFP, no BRD, no weighted scorecard. Maribel has not stated whether Touro will run a formal evaluation, shortlist vendors, or issue an RFI/RFP. The Salesforce Business_Requirements__c and Top_Challenges_Priorities__c fields are not populated. The criteria inferred from the call are real but unconfirmed and not decision-ready. The next move is to ask Maribel on the follow-up call: 'Are you planning a formal RFP or vendor comparison, and if so, what are the top 3-5 must-haves Touro will score us on?' This will surface whether the deal is heading toward a structured process or remains informal market research.

items.name: Maribel Giraldo
items.risk: Weak champion. Engaged in discovery but not advocating internally. Expressed skepticism about Zycus's limited higher-ed presence. Has not introduced Justin to EB/DM or other stakeholders. Will cool if higher-ed references and pricing are not delivered within 7-14 days.
items.title: Director of Procurement Services

items.detail: Lack of higher-education customer references is the PRIMARY vulnerability. Maribel explicitly stated 'I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education' (2026-06-16 call). This is a trust/credibility gap, not a product gap. Western Governors University is the only publicly referenceable higher-ed customer Justin could name. Without 2-3 additional named multi-campus university references, this deal will not progress beyond competitive-intel. This is the single highest-priority blocker.

Pricing transparency required. Maribel asked 'would you be able to share pricing even if it's superficial pricing per module?' (2026-06-16 call). She needs modular + user-count clarity to evaluate cost feasibility against Unimarket. Justin did not provide pricing on the call (said it depends on user count and modules), so this is an open requirement. Without it, Maribel cannot build an internal business case or compare Zycus to alternatives.

Adoption and change management across siloed campuses is a secondary vulnerability. Maribel noted: 'We are just very big and still operating in silos... We've done demos for the contract module, but no traction yet' (2026-06-16 call). Touro has a history of low adoption (Unimarket CLM module unused), so any new platform will face the same siloed-campus resistance unless positioned as a non-disruptive overlay (e.g. Merlin on top of Unimarket, campus-by-campus rollout). This is a positioning opportunity, not a product gap.

Incumbent inertia. Unimarket has been deployed for 8-9 years ('since I've been here, so I inherited') and 'serves a purpose' (2026-06-16 call). Maribel is not driving a replacement; she is gathering competitive intelligence. Without a quantified pain, a champion, and a finance/ops pain-owner, the status quo will win. This is the primary competitor.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole: Evaluator; Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16
champion_strength.summary: Weak champion. Maribel Giraldo is engaged (hosted discovery call, asked detailed questions, requested follow-up materials) but is not advocating for Zycus internally. She has not introduced Justin to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or campus stakeholders, has not shared internal documents, and has not pushed back on competitors. Her mandate is market research, not a replacement initiative. She expressed a real barrier (lack of higher-ed references) but is not actively working to overcome it on Zycus's behalf. She is a coach (helping Justin understand Touro's concerns) but not yet a champion (advocating for Zycus). At risk of cooling if Justin does not address her two blockers (references and pricing) within 7-14 days.

north_star_verdict.math: Deal created 2026-06-17, 11 days old. Currently in Qualified stage with a close date of 2027-02-18 (235 days out). Time-in-stage is 11 days against a typical 12-15 month enterprise cycle, so stage duration is normal for discovery. No forward slip detected (deal is new). However, the pace required to reach Vendor Selected or Negotiation by late 2026 (6 months) demands a compelling event, an executive sponsor, and multi-threading within the next 30-60 days. Current trajectory suggests competitive intelligence gathering rather than an active evaluation, which would push true closure into late 2027 or FY28 if momentum does not materialize. LastActivityDate is 2026-06-16 (12 days ago), within the 14-day fresh window, so engagement is still live but cooling without buyer follow-up.

north_star_verdict.headline: Discovery stalled after single call. Maribel expressed hesitation over lack of higher-ed references and requested pricing 12 days ago; neither addressed. No executive sponsor, no pain quantified, no compelling event. Market-scan posture, not active evaluation.

flags: Higher-ed reference gap is the PRIMARY barrier—Maribel stated 'I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education' (2026-06-16 call). Secure 2-3 named higher-ed references beyond Western Governors (ideally multi-campus universities) and position Merlin Intake as a non-disruptive overlay on Unimarket (campus-by-campus rollout) to lower the switching risk and create a wedge.

Single-threaded, intel-gathering posture—Maribel is the only contact, explicitly stated she is 'not looking to rip or replace' and is 'trying to learn about the competition' (2026-06-16 call). Convert her to champion by co-authoring a quantified rogue-spend business case (dollar impact, campus breakdown) and securing an introduction to a finance/ops stakeholder who owns the budget for procurement transformation within the next 14 days. Without a champion and multi-threading, this remains a competitive-intel exercise with no path to close.

No Economic Buyer or pain-owner identified—no CFO, VP Finance, CIO, or Executive Sponsor engaged, and no budget/approval discussion on the 2026-06-16 call. For a multi-campus university system, the EB is likely a VP Finance or Chief Procurement Officer above Maribel. Ask Maribel directly: 'If Touro were to invest in a Source-to-Pay platform, who would approve that decision and control the budget?' Secure an introduction to that person within the next 14 days or the deal will stall at discovery.

Single-threaded: only Maribel Giraldo (Director of Procurement Services, Evaluator) engaged. No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, CFO, Provost, or VP of Operations mapped or in the conversation. (source: SF OpportunityContactRole; Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16)

No quantified business case: Maribel described qualitative pain (siloed campuses, low CLM adoption) but no dollar-value cost, time savings, or ROI. Without metrics, no executive-level sponsorship. (source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16)

Higher-ed customer reference gap (PRIMARY BLOCKER): Maribel explicitly stated 'I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education.' Justin could name only Western Governors University. This is a trust/credibility barrier, not a product gap. Without 2-3 named multi-campus university references, deal will not progress. (source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16)

No pricing transparency: Maribel asked for module-level pricing on 2026-06-16 call. Justin deflected and has not followed up. This is an open requirement blocking feasibility assessment. (source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16; SF Next_Step__c does not mention pricing)

No decision process or timeline: Maribel did not describe how Touro evaluates vendors, whether a formal RFP is planned, or who else must approve. Without a process map, cannot predict stage progression. (source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16)
best_practice_check.summary: Five critical gaps block progression: (1) Single-threaded (only Maribel engaged, no EB/DM/exec sponsor), (2) no quantified business case, (3) lack of higher-ed customer references (primary blocker), (4) no pricing transparency, and (5) no decision process or timeline. These are standard early-stage discovery gaps, but the higher-ed reference gap is acute and explicitly stated by Maribel as a no-go. The deal is 12 days post-discovery with no follow-up from Justin on the two explicit requirements (references and pricing), so momentum is at risk of cooling. Immediate action required within 7 days.

competitive_position.summary: The primary competitor is Status Quo / Do Nothing. Unimarket is the entrenched incumbent (deployed 8-9 years, 'serves a purpose'), and Touro has no compelling event or quantified pain driving replacement. Coupa, JAGGAER, and GEP Worldwide are logged in Salesforce but were not mentioned on the 2026-06-16 discovery call. The wedge against Zycus is the lack of higher-education customer references ('very little representation within higher education'). The wedge FOR Zycus is Merlin Intake positioned as a non-disruptive AI overlay that can run on top of Unimarket to solve siloed-campus adoption pain without requiring a rip-and-replace.

items.quote: Maribel: 'Would you be able to share pricing even if it's superficial pricing per module?' Justin did not provide it on the call.
items.requirement: Provide module-level pricing, even if superficial, showing cost per module and user-count impact.

items.inferred_need: Send Zycus RFP template to Maribel so she can understand Zycus's recommended procurement evaluation framework and compare it to Touro's internal process.
items.grounding_quote: Maribel asked general discovery questions about Zycus's platform and customers. Justin responded by offering to send the RFP template (logged in Next_Step__c: 'Justin to send Zycus' RFP template'), which Maribel accepted.

items.inferred_need: Share screenshots of Merlin Intake and a Merlin overview so Maribel can visualize the AI-driven intake workflow and understand how it differs from Unimarket.
items.grounding_quote: Maribel asked about Merlin Intake functionality. Justin offered to send screenshots and an overview. Maribel agreed to review.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin Intake is in scope (SF Products__c: iSaaS;Merlin Intake), and Maribel asked about it on the 2026-06-16 discovery call, so AI positioning has begun. However, Justin did not articulate a strong Merlin value proposition tied to Touro's siloed-campus adoption pain. The opportunity is to position Merlin Intake as a non-disruptive AI overlay that runs on top of Unimarket and solves the low-adoption problem (CLM module unused) by automating intake workflows across campuses without requiring a rip-and-replace. This is the wedge that removes Maribel's risk concern and makes Zycus the safe, innovative choice. Under-positioned: yes. The AI story exists but is not yet anchored to Touro's specific pain.

items.quote: Maribel: 'I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education.' Justin could name only Western Governors University.

Maribel: 'Would you be able to share pricing even if it's superficial pricing per module?' Justin deflected, saying it depends on user count and modules, but did not follow up with even a rough range.
items.criterion: Modular pricing transparency (per-module cost, user-count impact)

items.quote: Maribel: 'We are just very big and still operating in silos... We've done demos for the contract module, but no traction yet.' Zycus can position Merlin Intake as a non-disruptive AI overlay on top of Unimarket, solving adoption pain without requiring rip-and-replace.
items.criterion: Ease of adoption across siloed campuses (low-disruption implementation)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Touro's expectations are directional but unquantified. Maribel articulated three criteria on the 2026-06-16 call: (1) higher-education customer proof (primary concern), (2) modular pricing transparency, and (3) ease of adoption across siloed campuses. Zycus is partially aligned on (3)—Merlin Intake can be positioned as a non-disruptive overlay—but exposed on (1) and (2). Without 2-3 named multi-campus university references and module-level pricing, Zycus will not progress beyond discovery.

hard.ais_why:
• Touro University's meeting showed 'positive reception to AI contract agent and AP Smartdesk features,' with interest from Maribel Giraldo 'in reducing rogue spend' and highlighted 'Autonomous Contract Agent' capability.
• Merlin Intake is scoped and the Agentic-platform-discussed flag is set to Yes, confirming AI features were engaged with positively.
• The compelling event is a Unimarket renewal roughly a year out, so the positive AI reception is not yet backed by near-term urgency, keeping it curious.
hard.next_step: Justin to send Zycus' RFP template.

pulse.summary: Live: last verified Salesforce activity 12 day(s) ago (2026-06-16).

deltas.to: Maribel Giraldo - weak
deltas.subject: champion
Provide module-level pricing and clarify user-count impact on cost.
Share higher-education customer references beyond Western Governors University.

Higher-ed reference gap is the PRIMARY barrier—Maribel stated 'I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education' (2026-06-16 call). Secure 2-3 named higher-ed references beyond Western Governors (ideally multi-campus universities) and position Merlin Intake as a non-disruptive overlay on Unimarket (campus-by-campus rollout) to lower the switching risk and create a wedge.

Single-threaded, intel-gathering posture—Maribel is the only contact, explicitly stated she is 'not looking to rip or replace' and is 'trying to learn about the competition' (2026-06-16 call). Convert her to champion by co-authoring a quantified rogue-spend business case (dollar impact, campus breakdown) and securing an introduction to a finance/ops stakeholder who owns the budget for procurement transformation within the next 14 days. Without a champion and multi-threading, this remains a competitive-intel exercise with no path to close.

No Economic Buyer or pain-owner identified—no CFO, VP Finance, CIO, or Executive Sponsor engaged, and no budget/approval discussion on the 2026-06-16 call. For a multi-campus university system, the EB is likely a VP Finance or Chief Procurement Officer above Maribel. Ask Maribel directly: 'If Touro were to invest in a Source-to-Pay platform, who would approve that decision and control the budget?' Secure an introduction to that person within the next 14 days or the deal will stall at discovery.

value.name: Maribel Giraldo
value.risk: Weak champion. Engaged in discovery but not advocating internally. Expressed skepticism about Zycus's limited higher-ed presence. Has not introduced Justin to EB/DM or other stakeholders. Will cool if higher-ed references and pricing are not delivered within 7-14 days.
value.title: Director of Procurement Services
value.source: SF OpportunityContactRole: Evaluator; Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16

value.name: Maribel Giraldo
value.risk: Single-threaded contact; if Maribel does not convert to champion and multi-thread to EB/finance, this deal will remain a competitive-intel exercise with no path to close.
value.title: Director of Procurement Services
value.sentiment: Neutral—engaged and responsive on the discovery call, but explicitly stated 'not looking to rip or replace' and is gathering competitive intelligence, not advocating for Zycus.

packets.subject: Maribel Giraldo

value.name: Unimarket
value.quote: Deployed 8-9 years, 'serves a purpose,' but CLM module has 'no traction' despite demos.
value.how_we_win: Highlight Unimarket's low adoption (CLM module unused) as proof that the incumbent is not solving Touro's siloed-campus challenge. Position Merlin Intake as the missing layer that drives cross-campus adoption without requiring Touro to abandon their Unimarket investment.

packets.subject: Unimarket

value.name: Status Quo / Do Nothing
value.quote: Unimarket 'serves a purpose' and has been deployed 8-9 years. Maribel is conducting market research, not driving a replacement.
value.how_we_win: Create a compelling event by quantifying the cost of siloed procurement (maverick spend, cycle time, contract leakage) and positioning Merlin Intake as a low-risk overlay that delivers AI-driven efficiency without disrupting Unimarket. Provide 2-3 named multi-campus university references who replaced legacy systems with Zycus and realized ROI.

packets.subject: Status Quo / Do Nothing

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Listed in SF Competitors__c field but not mentioned on the 2026-06-16 call.
value.how_we_win: If Coupa enters the conversation, position Zycus as the higher-ed specialist with deeper P2P functionality and Merlin AI differentiation. Stress that Coupa is a horizontal platform without vertical depth in university procurement.

packets.subject: Coupa

value.name: JAGGAER
value.quote: Listed in SF Competitors__c field but not mentioned on the 2026-06-16 call.
value.how_we_win: If JAGGAER enters, stress Zycus's Merlin AI advantage and multi-campus deployment success in higher-ed (if references can be secured). Position JAGGAER as a legacy platform without modern AI-driven intake and orchestration.

packets.subject: JAGGAER

value.name: GEP Worldwide
value.quote: Listed in SF Competitors__c field but not mentioned on the 2026-06-16 call.
value.how_we_win: If GEP enters, position Zycus as the SaaS-native, AI-first alternative. Stress GEP's consulting-heavy model and higher total cost of ownership vs. Zycus's self-service iSaaS + Merlin.

packets.subject: GEP Worldwide

value.name: Workday
value.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16, Maribel Giraldo (market-awareness context, no active evaluation)
value.how_we_win: Not a live threat; monitor for any signs of Workday entering the conversation. Focus effort on Unimarket displacement and status-quo conversion.

value.flag: Single-threaded, intel-gathering posture—Maribel is the only contact, explicitly stated she is 'not looking to rip or replace' and is 'trying to learn about the competition' (2026-06-16 call). Convert her to champion by co-authoring a quantified rogue-spend business case (dollar impact, campus breakdown) and securing an introduction to a finance/ops stakeholder who owns the budget for procurement transformation within the next 14 days. Without a champion and multi-threading, this remains a competitive-intel exercise with no path to close.

packets.subject: Single-threaded, intel-gathering posture—Maribel is the only contact, explicitly stated she is 'not looking to rip or replace' and is 'trying to learn about the competition' (2026-06-16 call). value.flag: No Economic Buyer or pain-owner identified—no CFO, VP Finance, CIO, or Executive Sponsor engaged, and no budget/approval discussion on the 2026-06-16 call. For a multi-campus university system, the EB is likely a VP Finance or Chief Procurement Officer above Maribel. Ask Maribel directly: 'If Touro were to invest in a Source-to-Pay platform, who would approve that decision and control the budget?' Secure an introduction to that person within the next 14 days or the deal will stall at discovery.
